page: Amazon Web Services r7g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv8 architecture with AWS Neoverse-V1 cores, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. It lacks hyperthreading, meaning each core runs a single thread. The CPU subsystem includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7g.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score. Memory bandwidth tests show high throughput, reaching 4644.96 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 253.07 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-core processing is also a strength, with strong results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests, falls into the average range.

The instance shows high efficiency in database and memory-bound operations, achieving 77.38 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and strong performance in PassMark database operations. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 64.66 GB/sec. However, text generation for very small LLM models is relatively weak, though text generation for medium models is strong. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its power profile, making it a viable alternative to x86 memory-optimized instances. The r7g.12xlarge is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases like Redis, relational database management systems, large-scale data caching, static web serving, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated ARMv8-based AWS Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It provides 384.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 253.07 GB/sec and a strong PassMark memory score. Multi-core benchmarks, such as software compilation and Redis SET operations (77.38 million operations per second), show strong performance, while single-core metrics remain average. With a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, relational databases, and multi-threaded compilation tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in Redis SET operations and database benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation
